generations.text:
[
  {
    "card_title": "Gold Mining Adventures at Mt Crichton Loop Track",
    "venue_name": "Mt Crichton Loop Track",
    "hook": "A loop filled with 'treasure' like a hidden rock tunnel and an authentic old miner’s hut.",
    "context": "This area was a hub during the Otago Gold Rush; the Sam Summers Hut offers a real glimpse into the 1930s prospecting life and is a significant piece of local heritage.",
    "practical": "Located on Glenorchy-Queenstown Road. The 8km loop takes about 2 hours. It is free to access with a designated car park across the road from the trailhead.",
    "insight": "The rock tunnel is safe to walk through and usually the highlight for kids—bring a small torch to make it feel like a real expedition.",
    "consider": "The track can be slippery near the waterfall, especially with the passing showers forecast for Saturday; sturdy footwear is a must."
  },
  {
    "card_title": "Family Strolls at Queenstown Gardens Lakefront Loop",
    "venue_name": "Queenstown Gardens Lakefront Loop",
    "hook": "The easiest way to get a 'big mountain' feel without leaving the town center.",
    "context": "Established in 1867, these gardens are the heart of the community and home to some of the oldest exotic trees in the region, including massive Redwoods.",
    "practical": "A 3.1km flat loop that takes 45-60 minutes. Access it from the end of Marine Parade. Free 4-hour parking is available at the gardens car park.",
    "insight": "Keep an eye out for flying discs—the gardens host a famous disc golf course. If the kids have extra energy, the massive lakefront playground is right at the entrance.",
    "consider": "It is a very popular spot and can get crowded on weekends; stick to the outer loop for the best views of The Remarkables."
  },
  {
    "card_title": "Turquoise Waters at Bob’s Cove Track",
    "venue_name": "Bob’s Cove Track",
    "hook": "A short bush walk that leads to a hidden jetty and some of the clearest water in the Wakatipu Basin.",
    "context": "Once a limestone quarrying site, this cove is now a peaceful local favorite for swimming and nature photography away from the CBD bustle.",
    "practical": "Located 15 minutes from town on the road to Glenorchy. The walk to the Picnic Point viewpoint takes about 30 minutes. Toilets are available near the beach.",
    "insight": "Locals love the wooden jetty for photos, but for kids, the rocky beach is perfect for skipping stones. The water here stays remarkably calm even when the lake is choppy.",
    "consider": "The final climb to the lookout is short but quite steep; with overcast weather predicted, the water may look less turquoise than in photos, but the bush remains lush."
  }
]
